have.d: a word that shows you own or possess something

has.e: The girl has a new toy.

had.d: to possess something in the past
had.e: I had a great time at the park.

her.e: The girl played with her doll.

how.d: How is a word used to ask about the way something is done.
how.e: How do you ride a bike?

him.d: Him is a word used to talk about a male person or animal.
him.e: The boy is playing with his ball. I can see him.

here.e: The dog is sleeping here on the rug.

high.d: being far up from the ground
high.e: The tall tree has high branches.

home.d: the house or place where you live
home.e: My family lives in a home with a big backyard.

help.d: to assist someone in doing something
help.e: The teacher will help me with my homework.

house.e: My house is big with a yard to play in.

however.e: I wanted to go outside, however it was raining.

having.e: I am having a great day at the park.

head.d: The top part of your body where your brain is
head.e: I touched my head to feel my hair.

hard.e: The math homework was really hard for me.

half.d: one of two equal parts of something
half.e: I ate half of my sandwich for lunch.

hand.d: the part of your arm you use to hold things
hand.e: I raised my hand to ask the teacher a question.

health.d: the condition of your body and how well it works
health.e: Exercising and eating healthy foods keeps my body healthy.

hope.e: I hope I can go to the park with my friends this weekend.

human.d: a person, not an animal or robot
human.e: As a human, I need food, water, and shelter to survive.

history.d: Stories and events that happened in the past
history.e: In history class, we learned about how people lived long ago.

hours.d: The amount of time it takes for something to happen
hours.e: It takes a few hours to drive to my grandma's house.

hit.d: To strike or collide with something
hit.e: I hit the ball and it went far.

heart.d: the organ inside your chest that pumps blood
heart.e: My heart beats faster when I exercise.

happy.e: The little girl was happy to get a new toy.

heard.d: To listen and understand what someone said
heard.e: I heard the bird singing outside my window.

held.d: To keep something in your hand or arms
held.e: The teacher held the book up for the class to see.

himself.d: The male person being talked about
himself.e: The boy cleaned his room by himself.

hear.d: To use your ears to listen to a sound
hear.e: I can hear the birds singing outside my window.

higher.e: The mountain is higher than the hill.

hey.d: A way to get someone's attention
hey.e: Hey, can you help me with this puzzle?

hold.d: To keep something in your hands
hold.e: Can you hold my book while I tie my shoes?

happened.d: When something takes place or comes about.
happened.e: A lot of fun things happened at the birthday party.

hour.d: A measure of time that is 60 minutes long.
hour.e: The movie was two hours long.

hands.d: The part of your body from your wrist to your fingers
hands.e: I use my hands to write, draw, and play games.

happen.e: I'm glad the good things I hoped for finally happened.

hot.e: The stove is hot, so be careful.

hate.d: A very strong dislike for something or someone
hate.e: I hate it when my little brother takes my toys without asking.

hi.d: A friendly way to say hello

hospital.d: A place where sick people go to get better
hospital.e: The doctor at the hospital helped my brother feel better.

hair.d: The thin strands that grow on a person's head
hair.e: I love to brush my long, brown hair.

holy.d: very important and special, often used for religious things
holy.e: The church is a holy place of worship.

huge.d: very big, much larger than normal
huge.e: The huge elephant stomped through the jungle.

hotel.d: A place where people stay when they are traveling
hotel.e: We stayed at a nice hotel during our vacation.

husband.d: the man a woman is married to
husband.e: The husband helped his wife with the chores around the house.

happens.d: When something takes place or is going on

heavy.d: Very big and difficult to lift or move
heavy.e: That box is too heavy for me to carry.

hurt.d: To feel pain or be injured
hurt.e: I hurt my knee when I fell down.

highly.e: I highly recommend reading that new book.

helped.d: To give someone assistance or support
helped.e: My mom helped me with my homework last night.

hall.d: A long room used for events or gatherings
hall.e: The school dance was held in the big hall.

heat.d: Warmth felt from the sun or fire
heat.e: The sun's heat made the day feel very hot.

highest.d: The most important or greatest of all
highest.e: Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world.

holding.d: To keep something in your hands or arms
holding.e: She was holding a bunch of colorful balloons.

hearing.e: Her hearing was good, and she could hear the bird singing.

ha.d: A sound used to show that something is funny or silly
ha.e: Ha, that joke was so funny!

healthy.d: Something that is good for your body.
healthy.e: Eating fruits and vegetables is healthy for you.

horse.d: A big animal that people can ride on.
horse.e: My cousin's horse is brown and very friendly.

handle.d: To hold, carry, or control something.
handle.e: I can handle carrying all my books in my backpack.

host.d: The person or thing that has guests or visitors.
host.e: My mom is the host for our family's holiday dinner.

helping.d: To give assistance or support to someone in need.
helping.e: The teacher is helping the student with their homework.

houses.e: My family lives in a nice house with a big backyard.

http.d: The way information is shared on the internet.
http.e: I can look up information on the http.

happening.d: Something that is occurring or taking place.
happening.e: A party is happening at my friend's house tonight.

henry.e: My friend Henry is coming over to play.

hundred.d: The number that comes after ninety-nine.
hundred.e: I have a hundred pennies in my piggy bank.

harry.d: A man's name, like your friend or neighbor
harry.e: My friend Harry likes to play sports.

housing.d: Homes and places for people to live
housing.e: My family has a nice housing community.

hello.d: a friendly way to greet someone
hello.e: I said hello to my teacher as I walked into class.

herself.d: a way to refer to a female person
herself.e: The girl did her homework by herself.

heads.d: The top part of your body with your face and hair
heads.e: The baby was bobbing his little head.

honest.d: Being truthful and not lying or cheating.
honest.e: My parents expect me to be honest about my actions.

hero.d: A person who does something brave or helpful.
hero.e: The firefighter was a hero for saving the cat from the tree.

haha.d: a way to show you think something is funny
haha.e: Haha, that joke was really silly!

heaven.d: a special place where people believe good people go after they die
heaven.e: The child dreamed of going to heaven one day.

helps.d: To make something easier or better
helps.e: My mom helps me with my homework every night.

holiday.d: A special day when people do not have to work.
holiday.e: My family likes to travel to the beach on holidays.

honestly.d: to tell the truth or be real
honestly.e: I will honestly tell you what happened.

hits.d: When something makes contact with something else
hits.e: The baseball hits the window and breaks it.

hole.d: An opening or gap in something
hole.e: I saw a small hole in the ground where a mouse lives.

honor.d: To show great respect for someone or something
honor.e: We should honor our teachers for all their hard work.

hoping.e: I am hoping to get a new bike for my birthday.

hang.d: to attach something so that it is suspended from above
hang.e: I hang my coat on the hook by the door.

homes.d: the house or building where a person or animal lives
homes.e: All the families in our neighborhood have nice homes.

humans.e: Humans need food, water and shelter to live

hundreds.d: Hundreds means many, many things, like over one hundred.
hundreds.e: There were hundreds of people at the concert.

holds.d: To keep something in a certain place
holds.e: The shelf holds all my books.

harder.e: The math problems got harder as I worked through them.

hat.d: A piece of clothing that you wear on your head.
hat.e: I wear a warm hat in the winter to keep my head and ears cozy.

height.d: The distance from the bottom to the top of something.
height.e: My height is taller than my little sister.

hardly.d: Almost not at all or very little.
hardly.e: The baby could hardly keep her eyes open.

headed.d: Traveling or moving in a certain direction
headed.e: The birds were headed south for the winter.

heavily.d: A lot or to a great degree
heavily.e: It rained heavily all day long.

hockey.d: A sport played on ice with sticks and a puck
hockey.e: My favorite hockey team is the Penguins.

hidden.d: Something that is not seen or noticed.
hidden.e: The cat hid in the hidden spot under the bed.

hide.d: To put something in a place where it cannot be seen.
hide.e: The children hid their toys under the bed.

historic.e: Visiting the historic castle was exciting.

hong.e: Hong Kong is a busy city with many tall buildings.

hopefully.d: in a way that makes you feel good about the future
hopefully.e: Hopefully, it won't rain on our picnic today.

harm.d: When something or someone is hurt or damaged
harm.e: It's important not to harm animals or the environment.

heritage.d: The traditions and history of your family or culture
heritage.e: Learning about her Native American heritage was very important to the girl.

hearts.d: The part of your body that pumps blood
hearts.e: My heart was beating fast when I saw the surprise.

hungry.e: I'm feeling really hungry after playing outside.

heading.d: The title or topic of something.
heading.e: The book's heading was 'How to Bake a Cake'.

hanging.d: something that is attached to a wall or ceiling
hanging.e: The picture was hanging on the wall.

hunt.d: to look for and try to find something
hunt.e: We went on a hunt for the lost puppy.
